response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and open-source development experience with a focus on testability and user experience, but his background is heavily skewed toward web, mobile, and JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystems. While he has some exposure to C++ and low-latency systems, his direct experience with geospatial data pipelines, routing systems, or modern C++/Rust in production is limited. His open-source GIS work and distributed systems exposure are positive signals, but the role requires deeper specialization in routing tiling and navigation data.
strengths: Extensive experience with distributed systems and low-latency applications (e.g., DLang at Sociomantic, micro-services at Meta).
Proven track record in open-source geospatial projects (e.g., GISCollective, Green Map), demonstrating passion for mapping and data pipelines.
Strong problem-solving skills and willingness to learn new technologies, as evidenced by his diverse toolchain (C++, DLang, Node.js, etc.).
concerns: Primary expertise lies in JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystems, with limited recent commercial C++ or Rust experience.
No explicit mention of routing tiling, navigation data pipelines, or real-time traffic integration in past roles.
Lack of demonstrated experience with high-scale geospatial data processing or optimization for global routing systems.
